title: STEAM Mastery Challenge
description: Take on these 20 intermediate-level questions covering science, technology, engineering, arts, and mathematics. You have 5 minutes (remainTime = 300) to complete this mega challenge!

questions.questionText: Which common post-processing step often removes support structures or smooths rough edges in FDM 3D-printed parts?
questions.explanation: Sanding or filing is a standard post-processing method to remove supports and smooth surfaces in FDM prints.

questions.questionText: Which phenomenon occurs when an object vibrates at a frequency matching another object’s natural frequency, causing the second object to vibrate sympathetically?
questions.explanation: Resonance happens when an object is driven at its natural frequency, causing large amplitude vibrations.

questions.questionText: Which propulsion system in modern rockets combines liquid fuel and oxidizer in a combustion chamber to generate thrust?
questions.explanation: Liquid-propellant rocket engines mix liquid fuel and a liquid oxidizer under high pressure for combustion and thrust.

questions.questionText: Which algebraic property explains why x + y = y + x?
questions.explanation: The commutative property states that x + y = y + x for real numbers x and y.

questions.questionText: Which separation technique uses a porous membrane to separate substances based on particle size or molecular weight?
questions.explanation: Filtration passes a mixture through a porous material to separate larger particles from smaller or dissolved components.

questions.questionText: Which pigment is primarily responsible for absorbing light energy during photosynthesis in plants?
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll absorbs light in the blue and red wavelengths, allowing plants to capture energy for photosynthesis.

questions.questionText: Which machine learning method categorizes data into predefined groups using labeled training examples?
questions.explanation: Classification is a supervised learning task that assigns inputs to one of several predefined classes.

questions.questionText: Which term describes the apparent brightness of a star as seen from Earth?
questions.explanation: A star’s apparent magnitude measures how bright it appears to an observer on Earth, irrespective of its actual luminosity.

questions.questionText: Which reaction type occurs when two compounds exchange ions, producing two new compounds?
questions.explanation: A double displacement reaction involves two compounds exchanging ions to form two different compounds.

questions.questionText: Which branch of civil engineering involves studying soil and rock mechanics to ensure stable foundations and earthworks?
questions.explanation: Geotechnical Engineering focuses on the behavior of earth materials for safe and stable foundations, slopes, and other geostructures.

questions.questionText: Which type of polygon has all sides of equal length and all interior angles equal?
questions.explanation: A regular polygon is equilateral (all sides equal) and equiangular (all angles equal).

questions.questionText: Which quantity is defined as the rate of change of velocity with respect to time?
questions.explanation: Acceleration is dv/dt, the rate at which velocity changes over time.

questions.questionText: Which global phenomenon involves a periodic warming of sea-surface temperatures in the central and eastern tropical Pacific Ocean?
questions.explanation: El Niño is characterized by warmer-than-average sea-surface temperatures in the equatorial Pacific, influencing global climate patterns.

questions.questionText: Which protocol ensures reliable data transmission over the internet by handling error checking and retransmission of lost packets?
questions.explanation: TCP provides reliable, ordered, and error-checked delivery of data between applications running on hosts communicating over an IP network.

questions.questionText: Which cybersecurity measure encodes data so only authorized parties can decode and access it?
questions.explanation: Encryption encodes data using a key so that only those with the corresponding key can decrypt and read it.

questions.questionText: Which algorithm repeatedly swaps adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order, resulting in O(n²) worst-case complexity?
questions.explanation: Bubble Sort operates by comparing and swapping adjacent elements, leading to a worst-case of O(n²).

questions.questionText: Which nitrogenous base is found in RNA but not in DNA?
questions.explanation: Uracil is present in RNA, whereas thymine is found in DNA.

questions.questionText: Which pollutant, primarily from coal combustion, contributes to acid rain when it reacts with water vapor in the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Sulfur dioxide (SO₂) can form sulfuric acid in the atmosphere, leading to acid rain.

questions.questionText: Which element is the lightest alkali metal, located in group 1 of the periodic table?
questions.explanation: Lithium (Li) is the lightest alkali metal, found at the top of group 1 in the periodic table.

questions.questionText: Which renewable energy source uses organic matter, such as plant or animal waste, to produce energy?
questions.explanation: Biomass energy is derived from organic materials (e.g., wood, crop waste, manure) to generate heat or electricity.

rewards.description: Earn 1000 points for conquering 20 STEAM questions!
